symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive confusion / altered mental status over 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fluctuating cognition/consciousness is the core feature of HE in known cirrhosis.
Altered mental status in a coagulopathic, fall-prone patient must trigger consideration of a structural bleed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inverted sleep-wake cycle (nocturnal wakefulness, daytime somnolence)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reversed sleep-wake pattern is an early, characteristic feature of low-grade HE.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced level of consciousness — rousable only to loud stimuli (clinician GCS 13)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progression to Grade 3 (rousable only to voice) marks advancing HE and airway risk — the trigger for emergency escalation. The lay report of drowsiness is elicitable via telehealth; the formal GCS is clinician-measured and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Asterixis (bilateral flapping tremor on sustained wrist extension) with dysarthria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Asterixis is a hallmark of metabolic encephalopathy; strongly supportive though not specific to HE.
A bilateral metabolic flap without focal signs points away from a focal structural lesion, though it does not exclude one.
Fetor hepaticus reflects portosystemic shunting and strongly supports a hepatic cause; it is an odour a clinician detects in person and cannot be assessed over chat.
Jaundice confirms significant hepatic dysfunction / decompensation; video-observable and caregiver-reportable, so admissible in telehealth as unverified.
Constipation increases colonic ammonia production/absorption and is a leading reversible precipitant of an HE episode.
Withdrawal of lactulose removes the principal defence against ammonia accumulation — a classic and readily reversible precipitant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ascites / abdominal distension with shifting dullness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ascites confirms portal hypertension/decompensation and creates the substrate for spontaneous bacterial peritonitis; visible distension is video-observable but shifting dullness requires examination.
Ascitic fluid is the site of SBP; diagnostic paracentesis is required to exclude it and cannot be done remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stigmata of chronic liver disease (spider naevi, palmar erythema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral stigmata corroborate established cirrhosis; assessed on in-person examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum ammonia (expected elevated, pending laboratory testing)

investigation_details.expected_result: expected elevated — pending laboratory testing; supportive only, not confirmatory, and never fabricated as a numeric value in AI-readable context
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: None for diagnosis — HE is a clinical diagnosis; a normal ammonia does not exclude it and a high value does not confirm it. Value is unknown and must not be invented.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ammonia correlates poorly with HE grade; supportive at best. Requires venepuncture — unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis or melaena observed by caregiver (GI-bleed screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult gastrointestinal / variceal bleed as precipitant
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A caregiver not having seen blood lowers but does not remove the probability of an occult bleed — melaena can be missed and slow bleeding is not always visible; cannot exclude remotely.
SBP is frequently afebrile and can present only as encephalopathy; absence of fever does not exclude it — paracentesis remains required.

symptoms.symptom_name: No recent head trauma and no focal lateralising weakness (structural-bleed screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No trauma or focal signs makes a bleed less likely but does not exclude a subdural in a coagulopathic patient; CT head is needed if no rapid improvement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Aspiration risk / unsafe swallow when obtunded

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hepatic encephalopathy with falling conscious level (airway at risk)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of airway-protective reflexes with reduced consciousness is the proximate life threat and contraindicates giving anything by mouth at home.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Altered mental status + inverted sleep-wake cycle + asterixis + fetor hepaticus in known cirrhosis is the characteristic overt-HE picture; the source note treats this constellation as clinically confirmatory.
Constipation plus lactulose non-adherence explain the acute decompensation and are the levers the definitive treatment pulls — but they do not obviate excluding a co-existing occult precipitant.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency in-person clinical assessment with conscious-level (GCS) monitoring and airway evaluation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A falling conscious level with aspiration risk needs hands-on assessment and airway monitoring the AI cannot provide.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — FBC, LFTs, UEC/electrolytes, coagulation/INR, glucose, venous ammonia, CRP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise hepatic decompensation, exclude hypoglycaemia/electrolyte derangement and coagulopathy, and support (not confirm) the HE diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Septic screen including diagnostic paracentesis of ascitic fluid
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Spontaneous bacterial peritonitis is a common, often afebrile precipitant of HE in ascites and must be actively excluded.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Assessment for gastrointestinal bleeding (PR examination, consider urgent endoscopy)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: GI/variceal bleeding is a leading precipitant; a caregiver not seeing blood does not exclude it.
Indicated to exclude a subdural/intracranial bleed if there is no rapid improvement or if any focal sign emerges, given cirrhotic coagulopathy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Isolated plasma ammonia used to confirm or exclude the diagnosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Ammonia correlates poorly with grade; it neither confirms nor excludes HE and should not gate treatment or disposition.
